column4: mechanics of Dam the Heart's River's own cost trigger (blood payment for a maneuver-strike chosen when range is set); fails transfer — no other card conditions a cost this way. Nearest 1.7, which owns cost timing generally but not this card's own trigger.
!TENSION:no-effect-plays a prohibition bars playing prevention cards even for zero net effect (cycling) — same slug as Blood Fury.6/Draba.3 in calibration.md.

restates Deep Cover Agent's own burn-option condition ("all your Seraphs in torpor" = "no ready Seraph"); fails transfer — no other card in this corpus shares this exact condition. Nearest 5.7 (Seraph as special class), which doesn't own ability-activation conditions.

!TENSION:no-effect-plays clarifies the burn-option condition is exactly as printed (no ready Seraph), independent of whether a valid target exists — an ability-activation instance of the no-effect-plays question, same polarity as Draba.3 (permitted despite no effect).

layered/overriding effects: when a later effect changes the acting minion in combat, the most recent one governs. No section owns this precedence rule — 2.4 owns ordering of simultaneous effects, not which of two non-simultaneous conflicting effects on the same trait takes precedence. Nearest 2.4. (Same gap recurs at Derange.7, this chunk.)

restates Derange's own targeting restriction (cannot move it to a vampire already of the target clan); fails non-obviousness — follows directly from the card's printed clan-change effect. No section owns single-card targeting restrictions like this; nearest 1.6.

sect as a trait: Derange changes clan but explicitly not sect, but nothing in the taxonomy owns sect as a standalone trait (5.8 owns sect change via titles, 5.7 owns sect-derived traits like Black Hand). Already on the watch list (Galaric's Legacy.1); this is a second instance. Nearest 5.8,5.7.

same precedence gap as Deep Song.2 (this chunk): when a later effect also changes clan (or another trait), the most recent takes precedence. No section owns general effect-precedence/override; 2.4 covers ordering of simultaneous effects only. Nearest 2.4.

Diabolic Lure's own pact-counter/pool-burn mechanic; fails transfer — the "counters reach capacity" condition is unique to this card's pact system. Nearest 1.7 (costs), which doesn't own pact-specific triggers.

restates that Diamond Thunderbolt (a cancel effect) does not also extend a temporary control effect's duration; fails non-obviousness — a cancel card is not an extension effect, so this follows directly from what the card does. Nearest 6.2, which doesn't own this specific non-interaction.

restates the bracket-notation convention that a capitalized discipline symbol requires superior level; fails non-obviousness — any judge fluent with discipline-level notation already knows this. Nearest 1.6.
!TENSION:no-effect-plays playable although it will do nothing; the prohibition cases (eg. Dead Hand.2, this chunk) point the other way.

!WORDING the ruling's phrasing is garbled ("cannot be used when a card is played to draw a card that would cancel it as it is played") — labeled as written; flagging for a check against the original ruling.

specific incompatibility between Echo of Harmonies' retained political action and Charming Lobby; fails transfer — no other card pairing is affected by this restriction. Nearest 3.7.5, which owns referendum procedure generally but not this specific card interaction.

specific numeric interaction between Eldest Command Undeath and Voter Captivation (counter split 2/0); fails transfer — the split amounts are this card pairing's own text. Nearest 3.7.5 (referendum) / 6.5 (pool), neither of which owns this specific interaction.
